# Jac Mobile - Architecture, Build, and CI/CD

This is the canonical guide for building Jac apps as mobile apps. It
covers the architecture, local development, the GitHub Actions pipeline,
release signing, backend URL injection, and what's outstanding for iOS.

If you're new, read **Overview** + **Architecture** to get oriented, then
jump to whichever section matches what you're doing.

---

## Overview

A Jac mobile app is a **React Native + Expo** wrapper around a
**WebView** that loads the Jac web bundle. The native shell is the same
for every Jac project; only the bundled web app changes. The pipeline
builds APKs on a GitHub Actions runner - no Expo Application Services
(EAS) Cloud is involved.

| | |
|---|---|
| Native shell | React Native + Expo (managed workflow) |
| Render target | WebView loading pre-bundled HTML |
| Build platform | GitHub Actions `ubuntu-latest` for Android, `macos-latest` for iOS (pending) |
| Signing | Real keystore from secrets if present, else auto-generated throwaway keystore |
| Distribution today | Downloadable APK artifact from workflow run |
| iOS status | Not implemented yet - see [iOS section](#ios--what-remains-to-be-set-up) |

---

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Source (committed)                                                │
│  jac-client/jac_client/examples/all-in-one/                       │
│  ├── main.jac, pages/, components/  ← your Jac app source         │
│  ├── jac.toml                       ← [mobile] config             │
│  └── mobile/                        ← native shell (auto-scaffolded)│
│      ├── app/index.tsx                                            │
│      ├── app.json, package.json                                   │
│      └── assets/jac-app/create-bundle.js                          │
└────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────┘
                             │
        jac build --client mobile -p android -b preview
                             │
                             ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Build directory (gitignored)                                      │
│  .jac/mobile/                                                     │
│  ├── (copy of mobile/ source files)                               │
│  ├── node_modules/                  ← from yarn install           │
│  ├── assets/jac-app/                                              │
│  │   ├── client.js, styles.css, index.html  ← Vite-built web bundle│
│  │   └── bundle.ts                  ← single-file HTML payload     │
│  └── dev-config.json                ← productionApiUrl baked here │
└────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────┘
                             │
            npx expo prebuild --platform android
                             │
                             ▼
                .jac/mobile/android/         ← Gradle project
                             │
                ./gradlew assembleRelease
                             │
                             ▼
       .jac/mobile/android/app/build/outputs/apk/release/
                 app-release.apk             ← uploaded as artifact
```

### Key components

**`mobile/` (source, committed)** - The React Native shell. `jac setup
mobile` scaffolds this once; you don't normally edit it. Contains
`app/index.tsx` (the WebView screen), `app.json` (Expo config),
`package.json`, and `assets/jac-app/create-bundle.js` (the script that
packs the web bundle into a single HTML string).

**`.jac/mobile/` (build artifacts, gitignored)** - Built by `jac build
--client mobile`. Contains a copy of `mobile/`, installed `node_modules`,
the compiled web bundle, and `dev-config.json`. `expo prebuild` writes
its native Gradle project here too.

**WebView screen (`app/index.tsx`)** - At app startup it:

1. `require()`s `dev-config.json` to read `productionApiUrl`.
2. `require()`s `assets/jac-app/bundle.ts` to get the single-file HTML
   bundle.
3. Does a literal string replace of `'%%__JAC_BACKEND_URL__%%'` in the
   bundle HTML with the resolved backend URL.
4. Loads the resulting HTML into a `<WebView>` with `originWhitelist=*`.

**`dev-config.json`** - Generated by `_write_build_config` (release
builds) or `_write_dev_config` (dev mode) in
`jac-client/jac_client/plugin/src/targets/mobile/dev_helpers.jac`. Holds
two fields:

```json
{ "localIP": "<for dev mode HMR>", "productionApiUrl": "<for release>" }
```

Metro statically resolves `require('../dev-config.json')` and bakes the
JSON content into the APK's JS bundle.

**`bundle.ts`** - Created by `create-bundle.js`. Inlines `client.js`
(as a base64 blob URL to satisfy CSP), `styles.css`, and the parsed
HTML into a single TypeScript module that exports `JAC_BUNDLE_HTML`.

### Why not EAS?

Removed entirely from this branch - the team prefers controlling the
build environment, the build steps, and signing rather than handing them
to Expo Cloud. The desktop pipeline already builds natively on the
runner (Tauri/Cargo) and this brings mobile to parity.

---

## Configuration - `jac.toml`

The `[mobile]` section drives everything. Generated by `jac setup
mobile` with sensible defaults; edit to taste.

```toml
[mobile]
name = "all-in-one"
bundle_id = "com.jaseci.allinone"   # iOS bundle ID + Android package
version = "1.0.0"
display_name = "All-In-One"
slug = "all-in-one"
scheme = "allinone"                  # for deep-linking

[mobile.platforms]
ios = true
android = true

[mobile.features]
use_sidecar = false
api_url = ""                         # ← Backend URL baked into the APK

[mobile.expo]
orientation = "portrait"
userInterfaceStyle = "light"
icon = "./assets/icon.png"

[mobile.expo.splash]
image = "./assets/splash.png"
resizeMode = "contain"
backgroundColor = "#ffffff"

[mobile.expo.ios]
supportsTablet = true

[mobile.expo.android.adaptiveIcon]
foregroundImage = "./assets/adaptive-icon.png"
backgroundColor = "#ffffff"
```

Most projects only need to change `name`, `bundle_id`, and
`[mobile.features].api_url`.

---

## Local development

Three local workflows depending on what you're doing:

### 1. Fast iteration with HMR (most common)

```bash
cd jac-client/jac_client/examples/all-in-one
jac start main.jac --client mobile --dev
```

- Runs Vite dev server on `:5173` + backend on `:9000` + Expo dev server.
- Scan the QR code with the **Expo Go** app on your phone (same Wi-Fi).
- Edit `.jac` / `.tsx` files → live reload, no rebuild.
- 90% of your work happens here.

For verbose backend logs in this mode:

```bash
JAC_MOBILE_VERBOSE_BACKEND=1 jac start main.jac --client mobile --dev
```

Picked up by `_start_backend_server` in
`jac-client/jac_client/plugin/src/targets/mobile/dev_helpers.jac` - pipes
the backend's stdout/stderr to your terminal instead of `/dev/null`.

### 2. Test the bundled app on device (no HMR)

```bash
jac start main.jac --client mobile
```

Same as above but loads the pre-built bundle instead of Vite. Useful to
verify the bundling step before pushing to CI. Still uses Expo Go.

### 3. Build a real APK locally (mirrors what CI does)

Prerequisites (one-time):

- **JDK 17** - `brew install --cask temurin@17` on macOS,
  `sudo apt install openjdk-17-jdk` on Ubuntu.
- **Android SDK** - install Android Studio, then export:

  ```bash
  export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
  export PATH="$PATH:$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin"
  ```

- **Node 20 + Yarn**.

Then:

```bash
cd jac-client/jac_client/examples/all-in-one

# 1. Prepare the bundle
jac build --client mobile -p android -b preview

# 2. Generate the native android/ project
cd .jac/mobile
npx expo prebuild --platform android --clean --no-install

# 3. Build a debug APK (no keystore needed)
cd android
./gradlew assembleDebug
# Output: app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk
```

Install via `adb`:

```bash
adb install -r app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk
```

> **Debug APK caveat:** `assembleDebug` does not pre-bundle JS and
> assumes a Metro bundler is running. For a self-contained APK that
> behaves like CI's output, use the release build below.

### Release build locally (matches CI output)

```bash
cd .jac/mobile/android

# One-time per .jac/mobile/: generate a throwaway keystore
keytool -genkeypair -v \
  -keystore app/release.keystore \
  -storepass ciinstall -keypass ciinstall -alias ciinstallkey \
  -keyalg RSA -keysize 2048 -validity 10000 \
  -dname "CN=Local Dev, O=Me, C=US"

# Append signing config to gradle.properties
cat >> gradle.properties <<'EOF'
MYAPP_RELEASE_STORE_FILE=release.keystore
MYAPP_RELEASE_STORE_PASSWORD=ciinstall
MYAPP_RELEASE_KEY_ALIAS=ciinstallkey
MYAPP_RELEASE_KEY_PASSWORD=ciinstall
EOF

./gradlew assembleRelease
# Output: app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-release.apk
```

The keystore + properties persist until you re-run
`expo prebuild --clean`.

---

## CI/CD - GitHub Actions Android pipeline

The workflow lives at `.github/workflows/build-mobile.yml` and runs the
same build steps as the local release path above, on `ubuntu-latest`.

### What the pipeline does

1. **Provisions the runner** - JDK 17, Android SDK, Python 3.12, Node 20, Yarn.
2. **Installs Jac packages** - `pip install -e jac` + `pip install -e jac-client` from the checkout.
3. **`jac setup mobile`** (idempotent, skipped if `mobile/` exists).
4. **`jac build --client mobile -p android -b preview`** - prepares the
   bundle in `.jac/mobile/` and bakes `dev-config.json` with the resolved
   backend URL.
5. **`npx expo prebuild --platform android --clean --no-install`** -
   generates the native `android/` project.
6. **Configure release signing** - uses the real keystore from secrets if
   all four `ANDROID_*` secrets are set, else auto-generates a throwaway
   keystore via `keytool`. Appends a self-contained `android { signingConfigs
   { ciRelease … } buildTypes { release { signingConfig … } } }` block to
   `android/app/build.gradle` (re-opens the `android {}` extension rather than
   regex-patching Expo's layout, so it's robust to template changes).
7. **`./gradlew assembleRelease`** - produces the signed release APK.
8. **Uploads** the APK as `android-release-<run#>` (downloadable from
   the workflow run page).

### Triggers

- **Push** to `feature/mobile-cicd-pipeline` when this workflow or any
  `jac-client/**` file changes.
- **Manual** `workflow_dispatch` with two inputs:
  - `app_path` - defaults to `jac-client/jac_client/examples/all-in-one`.
  - `api_url` - backend URL to bake into the APK (see [Backend URL Injection](#backend-url-injection)).

### Signing modes

The pipeline always produces a release-mode APK (pre-bundled JS,
`__DEV__ = false`, installable on any device). What changes is the
signing identity:

| Source | Triggered when | APK is… |
|---|---|---|
| Real keystore from repo secrets | All four `ANDROID_*` secrets are present | Stable signing - Play-Store-distributable |
| Auto-generated throwaway keystore | Any of the four secrets is missing | Installable on any device for QA / sideload. **Cannot be uploaded to Google Play** - the signing identity changes every CI run, which breaks Play's update verification |

The build summary at the bottom of the run page tells you which mode was
used.

### Local repro of CI

The full CI flow is reproducible locally with the [release build locally](#release-build-locally-matches-ci-output)
recipe above. There's nothing CI-specific in the build itself - it's a
plain `gradlew assembleRelease`.

---

## Backend URL injection

The APK needs to know where the backend lives. The URL is **baked in at
build time** via `.jac/mobile/dev-config.json`, which the WebView screen
loads at app startup (`require('../dev-config.json')`) and uses to fill
the `'%%__JAC_BACKEND_URL__%%'` placeholder in the bundled HTML.

### Resolution order

`_resolve_production_api_url` in
`jac-client/jac_client/plugin/src/targets/mobile/dev_helpers.jac` picks
the URL from the first source that's set:

1. **`JAC_MOBILE_API_URL` environment variable** - wins if non-empty.
2. **`[mobile.features].api_url` in `jac.toml`** - fallback.
3. Empty string - the build still completes but warns, and runtime API
   calls will fail.

### Setting it in CI

The workflow exposes `api_url` as a `workflow_dispatch` input that maps
straight to `JAC_MOBILE_API_URL`:

- Manual run: Actions tab → Build Mobile Apps → Run workflow → fill in
  `api_url` (e.g. `https://api.staging.example.com`).
- Automated push: the input is empty by default, so the build uses the
  TOML value. Set `[mobile.features].api_url` in `jac.toml` to a sensible
  default (typically staging).

### Setting it locally

```bash
# One-off:
JAC_MOBILE_API_URL=https://api.staging.example.com \
  jac build --client mobile -p android -b preview

# Or durable (direnv, shell profile, .envrc):
export JAC_MOBILE_API_URL=https://api.staging.example.com
```

### Verifying

```bash
cat jac-client/jac_client/examples/all-in-one/.jac/mobile/dev-config.json
# { "localIP": "", "productionApiUrl": "https://api.staging.example.com" }
```

If `productionApiUrl` is empty, the WebView will log *"Production API URL
not configured!"* on first launch.

---

## Production signing - generating a stable release keystore

The auto-generated throwaway keystore is fine for sideload testing, but
**not** for the Play Store. Generate a stable keystore once, **back it
up somewhere safe** (lose it and you can never publish updates), and
store it as GitHub secrets.

```bash
# 1. Generate a release keystore (do this once, locally, and back it up)
keytool -genkeypair -v \
  -keystore release.keystore \
  -keyalg RSA -keysize 2048 -validity 10000 \
  -alias <YOUR_KEY_ALIAS>

# 2. Base64-encode for GitHub secret storage
base64 -i release.keystore | pbcopy        # macOS
base64 release.keystore | xclip -sel clip  # Linux
```

Add four GitHub repo secrets (Settings → Secrets and variables →
Actions):

| Secret | Value |
|---|---|
| `ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64` | Base64-encoded keystore |
| `ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD` | Keystore password |
| `ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS` | The alias you passed to `-alias` |
| `ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD` | Key password (often same as keystore password) |

Next workflow run will detect all four and sign with your keystore.
Build summary will say `Signing: release-keystore` instead of
`throwaway-keystore`.

### From APK to AAB (Play Store requirement)

Google Play requires `.aab` (Android App Bundle). Swap the gradle
command in `build-mobile.yml`:

```yaml
./gradlew bundleRelease   # instead of assembleRelease
```

Output path becomes `app/build/outputs/bundle/release/app-release.aab`.
You can keep both an APK and an AAB by running both gradle commands -
APK for sideload, AAB for Play.

---

## iOS - what remains to be set up

iOS cannot be built on Linux runners; Xcode only runs on macOS. The plan
is to add a sibling `build-ios` job that runs on `macos-latest` once
Apple credentials are provisioned.

### 1. Apple Developer Program membership

- **$99/year** (individual) or **$299/year** (organization).
- Sign up at https://developer.apple.com/programs/.
- Organizations need a D-U-N-S number - adds 1–2 weeks. Plan ahead.

### 2. App ID

Register at
https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/identifiers/list with
bundle identifier matching `[mobile].bundle_id` in `jac.toml`
(e.g. `com.jaseci.allinone`).

### 3. Distribution certificate

- Easiest: open Xcode on a Mac, sign in with the Apple Developer
  account, let Xcode generate a Distribution certificate, then export
  from Keychain Access as a `.p12` file with a password.
- Alternative: `fastlane match` to manage certs in a private git repo -
  better with multiple developers.

### 4. Provisioning profile

- **Ad Hoc** (UDID-registered devices): download `.mobileprovision`
  from developer.apple.com.
- **App Store / TestFlight**: download the "App Store" profile.

### 5. App Store Connect API key *(only for auto-upload to TestFlight)*

- https://appstoreconnect.apple.com → Users and Access → Keys.
- Create with "App Manager" role.
- Download the `.p8` file (only available once - save it).
- Note **Key ID** and **Issuer ID**.

Skip if you'll upload via Transporter / Xcode manually.

### 6. Team ID

10-char string at top-right of https://developer.apple.com/account/
(Membership page).

### GitHub secrets to add

| Secret | Source |
|---|---|
| `IOS_CERTIFICATE_BASE64` | `base64 -i Certificates.p12` |
| `IOS_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD` | Password set when exporting the `.p12` |
| `IOS_PROVISIONING_PROFILE_BASE64` | `base64 -i profile.mobileprovision` |
| `IOS_TEAM_ID` | 10-char Team ID |
| `IOS_APP_STORE_CONNECT_KEY_BASE64` | `base64 -i AuthKey_XXXX.p8` (optional) |
| `IOS_APP_STORE_CONNECT_KEY_ID` | Key ID (optional) |
| `IOS_APP_STORE_CONNECT_ISSUER_ID` | Issuer ID (optional) |

### Workflow changes needed

Add a `build-ios` job alongside `build-android`:

```yaml
build-ios:
  name: Build iOS
  runs-on: macos-latest
  timeout-minutes: 90
  steps:
    # ... same Python / Node / jac install steps ...

    - name: Import signing certs
      uses: apple-actions/import-codesign-certs@v3
      with:
        p12-file-base64: ${{ secrets.IOS_CERTIFICATE_BASE64 }}
        p12-password: ${{ secrets.IOS_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD }}

    - name: Install provisioning profile
      uses: apple-actions/download-provisioning-profiles@v3
      with:
        bundle-id: com.jaseci.allinone
        issuer-id: ${{ secrets.IOS_APP_STORE_CONNECT_ISSUER_ID }}
        api-key-id: ${{ secrets.IOS_APP_STORE_CONNECT_KEY_ID }}
        api-private-key: ${{ secrets.IOS_APP_STORE_CONNECT_KEY_BASE64 }}

    - name: Prepare mobile bundle
      working-directory: ${{ env.APP_PATH }}
      run: jac build --client mobile -p ios -b preview

    - name: Generate native iOS project
      working-directory: ${{ env.APP_PATH }}/.jac/mobile
      run: |
        npx expo prebuild --platform ios --clean --no-install
        cd ios && pod install

    - name: Build IPA
      working-directory: ${{ env.APP_PATH }}/.jac/mobile/ios
      run: |
        xcodebuild -workspace *.xcworkspace \
          -scheme <SchemeName> \
          -configuration Release \
          -archivePath build/App.xcarchive \
          archive
        xcodebuild -exportArchive \
          -archivePath build/App.xcarchive \
          -exportPath build/ \
          -exportOptionsPlist ExportOptions.plist

    - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: ios-${{ github.run_number }}
        path: ${{ env.APP_PATH }}/.jac/mobile/ios/build/*.ipa
```

You'll also need an `ExportOptions.plist` (committed or generated) -
tells `xcodebuild` whether to package for Ad Hoc, Development, or App
Store distribution.

### Cost note

`macos-latest` runners cost **10×** the per-minute rate of
`ubuntu-latest` on GitHub-hosted plans. A 30-minute iOS build = 300
ubuntu-minutes of quota. Mitigations:

- Self-hosted Mac runner.
- Only build iOS on tagged releases, not every push.
- Cache `Pods/`, `~/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData`, `node_modules`.

---

## Local iOS build (Mac only)

```bash
jac build --client mobile -p ios -b preview
cd .jac/mobile
npx expo prebuild --platform ios --clean --no-install
cd ios && pod install
open *.xcworkspace   # opens Xcode
# Pick a simulator → ▶ Run, or pick your device with signing identity
```

For first-time setup let Xcode handle the signing identity, then
optionally drop to CLI:

```bash
xcodebuild -workspace *.xcworkspace -scheme <Name> -configuration Debug build
```

---

## Troubleshooting

**`jac --version` works locally but workflow fails to find it.**
The pip-installed entry points may not be on `PATH` in the next step.
Either invoke as `python -m jaclang` or ensure `setup-python` exports
its `Scripts` / `bin` to `GITHUB_PATH`.

**`expo prebuild` warns about plugins not installed.**
The `--no-install` flag we pass skips an extra `yarn install` because
`_prepare_build_dir` already ran one. If a plugin in `app.json` was
added after that step, drop `--no-install`.

**Gradle wrapper "Permission denied".**
The workflow `chmod +x ./gradlew` before running it. On a self-hosted
runner the checkout may have lost the executable bit - keep the
`chmod`.

**APK installs but the WebView is blank.**
The web bundle wasn't embedded. Check `Verify build artifacts` found
`assets/jac-app/bundle.ts`. If missing, an earlier Python error was
swallowed.

**Release APK build fails - `Could not get unknown property 'MYAPP_RELEASE_STORE_FILE'`.**
The `Configure release signing` step writes the `MYAPP_RELEASE_*` values
into `android/gradle.properties` and appends a signing block to
`android/app/build.gradle`. If the build step ran without the signing
step having run first (e.g. a re-run starting mid-job), those properties
won't exist. Re-run the whole job. The signing block is appended fresh
each run and guarded against double-append by a marker comment.

**APK installs once but won't update next CI run.**
Throwaway-keystore fallback was used. Each CI run generates a fresh
keystore → different signing identity → Android refuses the update.
Either uninstall before reinstalling, or configure the four `ANDROID_*`
secrets.

**Expo Go shows blank screen in `--dev` mode.**
Known issue with the jaclang client compiler when `sv import` pulls a
walker into a `cl {}` block - `compiled/main.js` doesn't export the
walker symbol, esbuild's lazy scan fails when the WebView loads. The
APK build path is unaffected (uses the pre-built bundle, not Vite). For
mobile dev testing, use `jac start --client mobile` without `--dev`, or
open the backend's `localhost:9000` URL in the phone's browser
directly.

**Backend dies silently in `--dev` mode.**
Run with `JAC_MOBILE_VERBOSE_BACKEND=1` to surface the inner
`jac start --port 9000` stderr instead of piping it to `/dev/null`.

**`No module named 'jac_client.plugin.config_loader'`.**
Cosmetic warning from `compiler.impl.jac` - a relative import that
falls back to a default `auth_redirect`. Doesn't affect the build.

---

## File map

| Path | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `.github/workflows/build-mobile.yml` | The CI workflow |
| `.github/JAC_MOBILE.md` | This document |
| `jac-client/jac_client/plugin/src/targets/impl/mobile_target.impl.jac` | `MobileTarget.setup/build/dev/start` |
| `jac-client/jac_client/plugin/src/targets/mobile/setup_helpers.jac` | Scaffold generators (`app.json`, `package.json`, WebView screen, etc.) |
| `jac-client/jac_client/plugin/src/targets/mobile/bundle_helpers.jac` | Web bundle → mobile bundle pipeline |
| `jac-client/jac_client/plugin/src/targets/mobile/dev_helpers.jac` | IP detection, `_write_dev_config`, `_write_build_config`, `_resolve_production_api_url`, `_start_backend_server` |
| `jac-client/jac_client/plugin/src/mobile_config.jac` | `[mobile]` TOML loader |
| `<example-app>/jac.toml` | `[mobile]` config section |
| `<example-app>/mobile/` | Scaffolded RN/Expo source (committed) |
| `<example-app>/.jac/mobile/` | Build artifacts (gitignored) |
